name: Improve scientific rigor and integrity across codebase and final paper

description: Baseline scientific audit: 6 rigor/integrity issues found across the final LaTeX paper and GSM8K evaluation script; established a reproducible audit metric for further iterations.

Hardened held-out GSM8K evaluation for rigor and reproducibility (greedy deterministic defaults, seed control, strict answer extraction, actual checkpoint_path loading, prompt-free decoding) and softened paper/report wording to avoid overclaiming training-set GSM8K results.
Corrected report/paper metadata from 'MTech CSE' to 'MTech DSAI' across the final submission files, preserving the improved audit score while fixing a factual affiliation error the user flagged.

Strengthened the scientific audit to cover the anonymous LaTeX paper, markdown paper, and submission checklist; the stricter audit surfaced 3 remaining scope-labeling issues that were previously untracked.
Removed the remaining scientific-scope labeling issues by aligning the anonymous LaTeX paper, markdown paper, and submission checklist with the held-out-vs-training-set caveat for GSM8K.

Tried compiling the final paper bundle and hit a LaTeX packaging failure immediately: reports/final/nips_style.sty depends on fullpage.sty, which is unavailable in this environment, so the submission is not currently reproducible from source.

Second compile attempt got past fullpage but failed on more non-core LaTeX dependencies in nips_style.sty (titling.sty missing). The style file is too fragile for a minimal TeX install and needs simplification to restore reproducible builds.

Compile progressed further but failed on another avoidable dependency: siunitx.sty is missing even though neither paper uses siunitx or algorithm packages. The TeX sources should drop unused packages to keep the submission reproducible on standard installs.

Compile now reaches document body but the custom nips_style maketitle override is incompatible with the paper's multiline author block, causing a LaTeX '\\' linebreak failure. The safest fix is to fall back to the article class's native title handling.

Compile failure root cause narrowed further: the main paper inputs supplementary_appendix.tex even though that file is a standalone document with its own \documentclass and preamble. The submission bundle is structurally inconsistent and needs the appendix compiled separately.

Main paper finally rendered, but BibTeX failed because plainnat bibliography style is declared twice: once in nips_style.sty and again in the paper. This blocks a clean reproducible build and should be resolved in the style file.

Made the final paper bundle reproducibly buildable on a minimal TeX install by simplifying nips_style.sty to core packages, removing unused paper dependencies, and compiling the supplementary appendix as a separate document instead of inlining a second preamble into the main paper.

Upgraded the scientific audit to include end-to-end LaTeX reproducibility checks for the main paper, anonymous paper, and supplementary appendix, while cleaning generated artifacts so the repo stays source-only after validation.

Strengthened the audit with new overclaim checks for 'confirms training stability' phrasing and ambiguous '99% GSM8K accuracy' statements; this exposed 6 additional wording issues but did not yet improve the repository state.

Tightened overclaim checks in the scientific audit and corrected the flagged wording by softening the 3-seed 'training stability' claim and clarifying that the 99% GSM8K result was a peak internal training-step metric rather than held-out benchmark accuracy.
